lines.en_lit: interpose or not interpose, said to him: All that the Sages instituted, they instituted parallel to Torah law.

Abaye said to Rav Yosef: partial entry, considered entry or not? said to him: The thumbs will prove as constitutes partial entry, and it was taught A leper immerses and stands at the Gate of Nicanor, A dilemma was raised before What is craft long knife and slaughter

Raise the dilemma to ben Zoma, raise the dilemma to the Rabbis who disagree with Rabbi Yehuda Raise the dilemma to ben Zoma: ben Zoma require only for inside however, for outside no, Or perhaps lest he come to be drawn

raise the dilemma to the Rabbis, who disagree with Rabbi Yehuda: the Rabbis say only there, where he is not performing service; however, here, where he is performing service, no, Or perhaps there is no difference, shall stand

Five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses The Sages taught Five immersions and ten sanctifications High Priest immerses and sanctifies on the day And all in the sacred in Hall of Parva, except for first which was in the non-sacred on the roof of Gate of the Water, and was alongside his chamber. Abaye said: Conclude from that Ein Eitam, twenty-three cubits higher than the ground of the Temple courtyard.

As we learned All the entrances that were there their height is twenty cubits and their width is ten cubits, except for to the Entrance Hall And it was taught “And he shall wash his flesh in water” the waters of a ritual bath. All of his flesh, in water that one’s entire body enters And how much is it? one cubit by one cubit by a height of three cubits. And the Sages calculated the waters of a ritual bath forty se’a.
